paper.title: Harnessing the Collective Intelligence of AI Agents in the Wild for New Discoveries (EinsteinArena)
paper.one_line: An agent-native open platform (open problems + deterministic verifiers + public leaderboard + discussion board) on which heterogeneous LLM agents asynchronously compete and collaborate in the wild, already setting 12 new math SOTAs including pushing the dim-11 kissing-number lower bound from 593 to 604.

original_question.text: Can a community of heterogeneous, autonomous LLM agents, given shared public infrastructure (a live set of open problems, a deterministic verifier per problem, a real-time leaderboard, and a discussion board) instead of a bespoke single-run harness, make substantially faster scientific progress than isolated agents -- specifically, can open public traces, iterative submission, and agent-to-agent borrowing of partial results extend the effective search horizon enough to produce verified new state-of-the-art results that no single agent found alone?

dominant_idea: The paper's load-bearing, treated-as-correct assumption is that scientific discovery can be operationalized as decentralized optimization against a public, deterministic, scalar verifier: 'progress' is identified with monotonically beating a leaderboard number on pre-curated problems whose objective is already a clean, cheaply re-computable float. Given that substrate, the claim is that the right intervention is simply to remove the bespoke harness and expose shared state (frontier solutions + discussion) so that more agents can borrow, compete, and refine -- and that observed leaderboard improvements (12 SOTAs, 593->604) are themselves the evidence that 'collective intelligence in the wild' works. Discovery = climbing a verifier; the social platform is the scaling lever; and the domains worth studying are exactly those where harm/value has already been reduced to a verifiable scalar an agent can hammer on offline.

clamped_dofs.statement: The object of study is locked to problems possessing a public, deterministic, cheaply re-runnable scalar verifier that maps a submission to a single float, with 'better' defined as monotone improvement of that number. The entire notion of 'discovery' and 'progress' is operationalized as crossing a leaderboard threshold on a pre-validated objective; problems are selected precisely because they ship a closed-form/efficient evaluation oracle (math optimization from AlphaEvolve), and verifiers are public so agents iterate offline and submit only credible improvements.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Section 2.3 makes the verifier 'the central artifact': executable Python returning one float, manually audited, public and reproducible. Section 2.2 curates problems with 'objective and computationally efficient evaluation.' The headline evidence (12 SOTAs, 593->604) is entirely numeric leaderboard movement. 'Scientific discovery' is thus silently equated with 'maximize a pre-existing scalar that a deterministic checker can certify offline' -- the goal function is inherited, not questioned.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If discovery were allowed to require problems with no clean verifier -- contested, multi-objective, non-commensurable, or qualitative value (formal proof correctness, biological plausibility, novelty/insight, real-world consequence) -- the question shifts from 'who climbed the number fastest' to 'how does a community of agents establish what counts as progress when no scalar oracle exists.' This attacks the core paradigm: the platform may be demonstrating efficient parallel optimization of pre-solved-in-principle problems rather than scientific discovery.

clamped_dofs.statement: The method is structurally confined to sandboxed domains with a re-runnable ground-truth dynamics/checker: solutions are verified in isolated E2B sandboxes by replaying the public verifier, and the whole 'iterate offline, submit credible improvement' loop presupposes a deterministic, replayable oracle. Domains where outcomes are noisy, irreversible, observational, or non-replayable (live markets, real experiments, open-ended proof) are placed out of scope.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Section 2.4 runs every submission through an isolated sandbox executing the verifier; Section 2.3 stresses local runs are 'semantically identical' to server-side ones. The Discussion explicitly concedes transfer to formal proof / algorithm design / computational biology is unestablished. The deterministic re-runnable verifier is the immutable precondition for the platform's feedback loop, never relaxed.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If verifiability were relaxed to partial/noisy/non-replayable outcomes, the platform would need observational or interventional inference (provenance, replication, peer adjudication) instead of brute-force re-execution -- a fundamentally harder and different problem. Releasing this would extend the 'collective discovery in the wild' claim to the open-ended domains the abstract gestures at, where the current design cannot operate.

clamped_dofs.statement: Credit, agency, and the unit of the experiment are fixed at the level of the individual agent and its discrete submissions/posts. The problem set, verifier code, threshold delta, proof-of-work gate, moderation policy, leaderboard acceptance rules, and the curators themselves are treated as the fixed, neutral game board -- never modeled as participants whose design choices shape outcomes.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Sections 2.2-2.6 set the platform operators as the ones who curate problems, write/audit/strengthen verifiers, pick the per-problem improvement threshold delta, run Llama-Guard moderation, and define acceptance rules -- all exogenous structure. Agents are the only entities that 'discover'; lineage (Fig 5) and topic analysis attribute progress to named agents (alpha_omega_agents, KawaiiCorgi, ClaudeExplorer). The designer/structure layer is unattributable by construction.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If the environment -- problem curation, verifier strengthening, threshold tuning, incentive/moderation design -- were treated as a co-author of the discoveries, the result could be reframed as 'platform engineering produced these SOTAs as much as agent intelligence did.' The notable 593->604 jump depended on verifier precision upgrades (80-digit Decimal) the operators made; surfacing this changes the claim from 'collective agent intelligence' to 'human-curated arena with agents as the search subroutine.'

clamped_dofs.statement: Success is validated almost entirely by leaderboard movement -- new top scores and the count of beaten SOTAs -- and the design lever is pre-baked as 'expose shared state so more agents can borrow and out-compete.' The diagnostic (best score per problem, SOTA count) and the prescribed mechanism (competition + public traces driving score-chasing) co-select for a story in which more sharing + more competition = more discovery.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Contributions 2-3 and Table 1 measure success as 12 improved scores; the leaderboard (Sec 2.5) and the competitive top-position pipeline are the central reward signal. The Discussion itself admits the leaderboard 'may bias agents toward short-horizon score chasing' and that competition can conflict with the information-sharing that actually produced the kissing-number result -- yet the success metric remains score improvement, foreclosing accounts where leaderboard pressure suppresses slow-but-deep directions.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If success were measured by robustness/generativity -- whether shared traces yield reusable insight versus noise, whether progress would recur under a different incentive structure, or whether the same gains arise without a competitive leaderboard -- the analysis might reveal that competition is net-harmful to discovery and that the SOTAs came despite, not because of, the scoreboard. This reframes the platform from 'leaderboard drives discovery' toward 'studying which incentive regimes actually produce cumulative knowledge.'

clamped_dofs.statement: The continuous proxy objective handed to agents is itself a fixed, expert-chosen relaxation of the true binary/qualitative question, anchored to a single reference framing (e.g. the kissing-number overlap penalty C(x)=sum max(0, 2-||...||) with C=0 = valid; discretization interval count as the lever). All optimization, lineage, and 'progress' are measured against this one surrogate, treated as ground truth rather than a degree of freedom.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Appendix A.1 substitutes a continuous overlap-penalty proxy for the binary kissing condition 'as suggested by AlphaEvolve'; A.2-A.5 fix discretized step-function formulations with chosen interval counts. KawaiiCorgi's winning move was switching to a Taylor-linearized least-squares surrogate -- i.e., exploiting the proxy's shape. The proxy and its discretization are inherited from prior work and never varied or stress-tested as choices that determine which constructions look good.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If the surrogate relaxation and discretization were treated as variables (multiple proxies, learned objectives, exact arithmetic throughout), the apparent 'discoveries' might shift -- some gains could be proxy-gaming (optimizing the relaxation, not the real problem) rather than genuine bound improvements. This would convert verified-against-our-checker claims into robustness-bounded claims and expose how much progress is an artifact of one expert-chosen objective shape.

clamped_dofs.statement: The well-posedness and integrity of the verified counterfactual is assumed: the public verifier plus threshold delta and proof-of-work are treated as a sound, gameable-only-fairly oracle, and the accuracy/feasibility of large collaborative runs is asserted from small or post-hoc evidence (lineage inferred by feature-vector similarity; verifiers patched only after agents expose edge cases) rather than validated at deployment scale.
clamped_dofs.why_clamped: Section 2.3/Discussion admit verifiers needed repeated strengthening (kissing-number precision exceeded double-precision and was upgraded to 80-digit Decimal post-launch) -- i.e., earlier 'verified' results stood on a checker later judged unsound. Lineage/parental attribution (Fig 5, App C) is reconstructed via similarity heuristics, not ground-truth provenance, and 'collaboration produced this' rests on these inferred edges. The soundness of the verifier-as-oracle and the validity of the lineage claims are presupposed, not independently checked.
clamped_dofs.if_released: If verifier soundness and lineage attribution carried error bars -- stress-testing whether accepted solutions survive a stronger checker, and whether 'agent A built on agent B' holds under provenance rather than cosine similarity -- some SOTAs and much of the 'collaboration, not isolated runs' narrative could weaken into 'concurrent independent search plus a checker that kept breaking.' This reframes the central empirical claim (largest gains emerged from agent interaction) from established to hypothesis-under-measurement-noise.
